ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT code 440370000 refers to satay generally; no chicken-specific satay code exists in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving of 10 chicken satay skewers, typically served with peanut sauce, is a substantial sharing portion weighing approximately 250 g.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Satay (with sauce)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for Chicken Satay (with sauce) sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo / Healthy 365 food calorie calculator.

gi_source.title: Chicken, roasted (analogous) / Peanuts (analogous)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index estimated at 45 based on grilled chicken (GI ~45) and peanuts (GI ~13), which are the primary components of chicken satay and its sauce. The high protein and fat content of satay further attenuates the glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as (45 × 7.2 × 250/100) / 100 = 8.1.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume chicken satay in moderation due to its sodium content from marinades and peanut sauce; requesting reduced-salt preparations and limiting sauce quantity can help manage sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~45) and high protein content (18.2 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Chicken satay has a low estimated glycemic index and a high protein content, making it a suitable option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in appropriate portions and with mindful peanut sauce intake to limit added sugars.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (2.7 g per 100 g) and sodium (300 mg per 100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular health, individuals should choose lean chicken cuts, limit skin and excess peanut sauce to reduce saturated fat and sodium load, and consume chicken satay as part of a balanced diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken satay is a popular Southeast Asian dish consisting of marinated, skewered, and charcoal-grilled pieces of chicken, traditionally served with a rich, savory peanut sauce (kuah kacang) and accompanied by compressed rice cakes (ketupat) and cucumber slices. The chicken is marinated in a fragrant blend of spices including turmeric, lemongrass, galangal, and coriander, giving it a distinctive golden colour and aromatic flavour. Grilling over charcoal imparts a characteristic smoky char. The texture is tender and juicy chicken with a lightly caramelised exterior, complemented by the creamy, nutty, mildly sweet, and slightly spicy peanut sauce. Originating from Indonesia and Malaysia, chicken satay is a staple street food and restaurant dish across Singapore and the wider Southeast Asian region. Nutritionally, it is a good source of protein (~18 g per 100 g) with moderate fat (~11 g per 100 g) and relatively low carbohydrate (~7 g per 100 g). Its estimated glycemic index is low (~45), making it a potentially suitable option for blood sugar management. Sodium content is moderate (~300 mg per 100 g), warranting attention for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease.
common_user_queries: Is chicken satay suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Nutrient data for Chicken Satay (with sauce) is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo, the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. GI is an analogous estimate based on approved University of Sydney GI Database values for primary components (grilled chicken, peanuts) with documented rationale.
notes: Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as they are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo source database. Chinese traditional form is null as the simplified form 鸡肉沙爹 is the standard representation used in Singapore. The input cuisine tag 'Chinese' has been corrected to 'Southeast Asian' as chicken satay is a Southeast Asian dish of Malay/Indonesian origin widely consumed in Singapore.
